cit.basis: Flat 21% corporate income tax (dan z prijmu pravnickych osob), raised from 19% by the 2024 consolidation package (konsolidacni balicek), effective from tax year 2024.

windfall_tax.note: Temporary windfall tax (dan z neocekavanych zisku): 60% surcharge on excess profits of large banks and energy companies for 2023-2025. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

cit.note: Administered by the Financial Administration (Financni sprava).

vat.note: 21% standard VAT (DPH); the 2024 reform merged the former 10% and 15% reduced rates into a single 12% reduced rate. EU One-Stop-Shop (OSS) available for intra-EU B2C distance sales.

personal_income_tax.note: 15% base rate; 23% on income above 36x the average wage (threshold lowered from 48x by the 2024 consolidation package).

social_insurance.note: Employer ~33.8% (social security 24.8% + health 9.0%); employee 7.1% social security (incl. 0.6% sickness insurance from 2024) + 4.5% health.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

metadata.pillar_two_note: Czechia has implemented the EU Pillar Two directive (Act No. 416/2023 Coll.): a 15% minimum top-up tax applies to members of multinational and large domestic groups with consolidated revenue above EUR 750 million. Out of scope for this SME estimator.
rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Windfall tax, Pillar Two top-up, investment incentives, R&D deduction, loss relief, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
